{"step_by_step_thinking": "Gingival abrasion and gingival ablation are both potential causes of gingival recession. Gingival inflammation is not a direct cause of recession but rather a contributing factor to periodontal disease, which can lead to recession. TFO (Tetracycline) is known to cause drug-induced gingival overgrowth, but it can also lead to gingival recession as a side effect. Therefore, the option that is not an etiological agent for gingival recession is C: Gingival inflammation.", "answer_choice": "C"}
